      acceptance_criteria: All non-runtime packet kinds can carry plan context without becoming plan authority; runtime resource target validation remains strict; a guard/test proves accepted operator vocabulary normalizes to typed refs instead of being dropped or stored raw.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-C` Collapse Codex/Claude visible state onto reducer-owned sync truth.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AE-E`, `MP377-P0-T22AJ-A`, `MP377-P0-T22AI-F`
      scope: Make `AgentSyncProjection` the shared truth for active packets, waits, ACK/apply/dismiss state, Claude status/ACK, dashboard, claude-loop, startup/status, and work-board rows; keep `agent-mind` as auxiliary diagnostics only.
      acceptance_criteria: Dashboard, claude-loop, sync-status, startup, and compact JSON agree on active packet and actor state; publisher refreshes cannot clobber Claude status/ACK because those fields have a typed writer; auxiliary mind hints cannot close work or grant authority.
      progress: 2026-05-01 projection-parity guard now treats `active_packet_id` as current-instruction authority and leaves `attention_packet_id` to the attention-specific checks, so blocked Claude sessions with pending packet attention no longer produce false queue/inbox current-instruction drift in `check_multi_agent_sync`.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-D` Add flow-level probes and sweep automation for Plan 4.1 failures.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T14`, `MP377-P0-T15`, `MP377-P0-T22AG-D`
      scope: Add probes/guards for packet plan-context flow, operator-vocabulary acceptance, event-id uniqueness (`rev_pkt_2620`), read-only command purity, command-result contract shape, provider registry parity, projection parity, lifecycle correlation, shim import mode, and tandem dogfood. Wire packet expiry/F-Root sweep into an explicit write cadence such as `review-channel --action expire-packets` or a scheduled workflow, while `status`/`sync-status` remain read-only and only surface stale-expiry debt.
      acceptance_criteria: `probe-report` includes event-id uniqueness; packet expiry debt is surfaced or repaired on a bounded cadence without hidden read-side writes; each recurring class has a guard/probe or a recorded `GuardPromotionCandidate` explaining why not.
      progress: 2026-05-01 first slice added `probe_event_id_uniqueness`, kept read-only status/sync/dashboard paths from hiding expiry writes, accepted operator anchor vocabulary as canonical typed refs, removed the `agent-mind` Codex/Claude parser whitelist so `cursor`, `operator`, `system`, and future provider ids reach runtime discovery, closed NC2 by requiring explicit route scope on non-runtime `action_request` posts, added `probe_command_result_contract` so command JSON envelope drift is visible through the normal probe-report lane, added a projection-parity regression for current-instruction vs packet-attention semantics, normalized source-identity observed fields when no pipeline generation exists, started Claude's `rev_pkt_2633` Phase A by adding `check_registry_path_integrity` plus registering `probe_event_field_naming_consistency`, added `check_provider_list_parity_graph` plus a shared provider registry so `agent-mind`, `monitor`, and future provider-aware commands cannot silently diverge, added `probe_inter_agent_communication_lag`, and changed `AgentLoopDecision` so read-only peer-packet triage preempts startup blockers without granting mutation authority.
      progress: 2026-05-01 follow-up closed Claude beta finding F8 by making bare `agent-mind --since-cursor` resume from the persisted typed agent-mind projection cursor when available, so cross-agent polling documentation and CLI behavior no longer diverge.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-E` Require live Codex/Claude tandem dogfood before Plan 4.1 handoff.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `queued`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-B`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-C`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-D`
      scope: Add a `plan41-tandem` dogfood scenario where Codex posts a scoped packet, Claude observes/ACKs/responds through typed surfaces, Codex observes and applies/dismisses/responds, and every involved projection agrees before `stage_commit_pipeline` or Plan 4.1 handoff.
      acceptance_criteria: The scenario records `devctl dogfood` / `governance-review` evidence; negative controls prove unscoped packets normalize or fail, stale bridge data cannot override reducer state, read-only commands do not write tracked files, and every packet reaches exactly one terminal outcome.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-F` Promote packet carry-forward and runtime-spine closure into typed attention state.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-C`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-D`, `MP377-P0-T22AM-F`
      scope: Add a reducer-owned `PacketContinuityState` / attention ledger that keeps ACKed-but-unresolved packets, apply-pending packets, and compacted-session carry-forward debt visible until terminal disposition or durable ownership in typed plan/intake/finding state. Operationalize the `SYSTEM_MAP.md` runtime-spine closure rule with `check_runtime_spine_closure.py`, and keep `CandidateInvariant` as the explicit missing finding-to-rule promotion contract so guard/probe lessons cannot stay prose-only.
      acceptance_criteria: ACKed packets cannot disappear from startup, sync-status, graph/plan prioritization, or handoff context merely because they left the pending queue; `probe_packet_carry_forward_debt` and the lifecycle-attention guard name unresolved packet ids and required next actions; `check_runtime_spine_closure.py` fails when any section 0.6 ❌/⚠️ object lacks the machine-checked closure matrix fields for active owner, typed contract, producer, consumer, regression proof, graph/context visibility, carry-forward path, and system priority; compacted or resumed sessions receive a bounded ContextPack/DevSessionPack summary of unresolved packets, runtime-spine gaps, active plan rows, and Claude/Codex watcher state.
      progress: 2026-05-01 `SYSTEM_MAP.md` section 0.6 now carries a machine-checked Runtime Spine Closure Matrix, and `check_runtime_spine_closure.py` rejects placeholder or missing owner/proof/visibility/carry-forward fields instead of accepting vague prose mentions.
      progress: 2026-05-01 `rev_pkt_2705` plus expired source packets `rev_pkt_2691`, `rev_pkt_2696`, `rev_pkt_2697`, `rev_pkt_2699`, `rev_pkt_2700`, `rev_pkt_2701`, `rev_pkt_2702`, and `rev_pkt_2704` were promoted from packet-only transport into durable MP-377 intake. The surviving packet is treated as a carrier and provenance anchor, not source-of-truth state. Durable follow-up rows now cover transition ack/apply/dismiss session disambiguation, clock-expired packet ingestion loss, agent-sync ambiguity projection, packet carry-forward debt, command hangs under fanout, and work-board row duplication.
      progress: 2026-05-01 Packet/work pressure now has a durable typed scaling policy instead of relying on chat or packet pileups. `DevelopmentScalingContract` treats near-TTL packet intent, carry-forward debt, inter-agent lag, independent plan slices, review/probe backlog, graph/system-map coverage gaps, and stale runtime windows as scale-out inputs; each added lane must carry a `WorkerPacket`, correlated replies, and durable plan/finding/run/ingestion evidence so packets remain transport and provenance.
      progress: 2026-05-01 Claude dashboard packet `rev_pkt_2708` exposed the missing creation-time binding contract. New `PacketCreationBinding` receipts now run from `post_packet` finalization for plan-scoped durable packet kinds, upsert `PlanRow` ownership at creation, attach the binding to reduced packet rows, and classify later TTL expiry as `expired_after_durable_binding` instead of carry-forward debt. Live packet `rev_pkt_2710` proved the hook by auto-creating `PKT-BIND-REV-PKT-2710`; `rev_pkt_2708` was backfilled through the same typed helper as `PKT-BIND-REV-PKT-2708`. Remaining Phase 1 debt is explicit: add `PacketDebtRemediationPipeline` to cluster and drain existing carry-forward rows plus a debt-growth/freshness probe so old packets get merged into plan/finding/lifecycle receipts instead of archived prose.
      progress: 2026-05-01 Packet-debt remediation now has a typed first pass instead of another prose queue. `PacketDebtRemediationReport` clusters bounded `packet_carry_forward_debt` rows, `PacketDurableIngestionReceipt` records durable backfill receipts, reducer rows attach `packet_durable_ingestion_receipt` / `durable_binding`, `PacketContinuityIndex` separates durable owners from carry-forward debt, `probe_packet_carry_forward_debt` names each required next action, and `devctl develop audit-packets --max-packets N` exposes the read-only controller view. The write-capable library path can backfill eligible finding/plan packets into `PlanRow` ownership; remaining closure is the guarded production drain/freshness policy that decides which legacy communication packets need lifecycle owners versus manual dismissal.
      progress: 2026-05-02 Claude slash-flow packet `rev_pkt_2725` (Bundle FF) proved the next user-facing packet-attention gaps. `/develop --status` now mirrors `packet_attention.required_command` as top-level `next_step_command`, includes the triggering packet in `pending_actionable_packet_ids`, and ranks the durable packet-owned row `PKT-BIND-REV-PKT-2725` before ordinary next slices. `review-channel --action show --packet-id <id>` now reads one packet body plus matching events through the typed surface, and `history --packet-id <id>` resolves the exact reduced packet row instead of returning the global history page. The validation split also removes the review-channel helper import-order cycle exposed by the new regression test. Remaining Bundle FF closure is caller-aware required-command rendering, packet-count conservation/renaming, inbox body previews, acked-but-queued queue visibility, and explicit archived-with-durable-owner disposition names.
      progress: 2026-05-02 Claude Bundles HH/JJ/KK shifted the active `/develop` finding from isolated packet UX gaps to the missing orchestration loop. `/develop watch` now composes typed runtime rows, peer `agent-mind` context, and raw provider session discovery into `DevelopmentLoopReport.runtime` / `peer_minds`: work-board and sync projections remain authority, while `agent-mind` is labelled auxiliary-only and can only suggest wake/read commands. The multi-session test proved provider JSONL streams can appear before the typed work board registers them, so Runtime now reports bounded `discovered_session_count`, `unregistered_session_count`, and session visibility rows. `/develop audit-packets --drain-packets` exposes the existing packet-debt plan-row ingestion writer for eligible rows and emits `PacketDurableIngestionReceipt` instead of leaving the recommendations read-only. Bundle LL (`rev_pkt_2732`) corrected the session finding to a spawn-to-registration lag, added `AckQueueAgingSignal` to the orchestrator input set, and recorded follow-up UX debt in pause/resume preview semantics, `tandem-validate` summary rendering, and the `view --surface ai` default mode. Bundle MM (`rev_pkt_2733`) then identified `agent-loop` as the existing orchestrator substrate: it already exposes loop identity, wake, proof, topology, authority, and action-request surfaces, while missing proof generators, event refresh, packet-attention/audit-packet ingestion, and writer-drive. Codex status packet `rev_pkt_2734` also exposed that provider-to-provider `system_notice` packets cannot yet carry `target_role` / `target_session_id`, leaving session-scoped non-plan communication unresolved. Remaining closure is to extend `agent-loop` into the typed `OrchestratorAction` / `WorkerPacket` writer path that consumes packet pressure, session-discovery gaps, dangling smartness inputs, ack-aging, proof gaps, session-scoped communication gaps, and safe-fanout gates automatically.
      progress: 2026-05-02 Codex/Claude wake dogfooding found a narrower typed-consumer gap: provider-to-provider `system_notice` packets were pending in queue/runtime state but invisible to `/develop` packet attention and suppressed by event-post wake as non-actionable. `/develop` now reports any live pending conductor-bound packet as delivery attention pressure via `latest_attention_packet_id` / `pending_delivery_packet_ids`, while preserving `pending_actionable_packet_ids` for instruction/action-request work; auto actor resolution can pick the single delivery-waiting actor before caller-env fallback. The later 2026-05-04 T22AN-AF correction supersedes the old delivery-wake wording: event-backed packet posts record typed attention only and still skip synthetic `operator` / `system` targets. Claude follow-ups `rev_pkt_2758` and `rev_pkt_2759` were ACKed and remain queued as broader orchestration-dedup / `TypedClosureConsumer` architecture findings rather than hidden chat state.
      progress: 2026-05-02 Follow-up dogfood packet `rev_pkt_2760` proved packet delivery attention by producing Claude response `rev_pkt_2761`, but it also made the human-facing boundary explicit: delivery attention does not prove the exact visible terminal resumed and must not be treated as launch authority. `/develop` now separates live packet delivery (`packet:<id>`), packet debt (`packet-debt-audit`), and checkpoint pressure (`checkpoint-required`) so the controller does not report ordinary startup authority blockers as inbox work. Remaining binding closure is now owned by T22AN-AF/T22AN-AG: packet delivery records typed attention, while scheduler/runtime controllers decide session starts at explicit task boundaries.
      progress: 2026-05-02 Claude finding `rev_pkt_2762` narrowed the root cause to the relaunch path: `maybe_wake_waiting_agent_conductor` used cleanup-candidate filesystem rows plus fresh conductor launch, not a registered actor/PID delivery channel. First closure landed: wake receipts carry `wake_method`, `target_role`, `target_session_id`, and replacement PID evidence when known; explicit non-Codex `target_session_id` packets now fail closed with `target_session_unreachable_without_registry` / `unreachable_until_operator_prompt` instead of spawning a different session and masking the failure. The exact `rev_pkt_2760` packet carried `target_session_id=6acbac54-7e3d-4cb8-a1a9-f55a43d48ce4`, so this branch would have prevented the observed ghost-spawn. Remaining work is the durable `ProviderConductorRegistry` / attach-method contract plus any provider-specific IPC or replace-only semantics; `spawned_pid` / `delivered_to_pid` are deliberately not reported until a real registry or launcher PID capture path exists.
      progress: 2026-05-02 Remote-control dogfood then split dashboard visibility from delegated execution. That slice allowed safe non-mutating dashboard/observer packets to use an explicit `headless_delegate` grant and persisted `PacketWakeReceipt` audit rows, but the 2026-05-04 T22AN-AF correction supersedes packet-delivery launch semantics: packet post now records typed attention only, and any visible/headless worker start requires separate scheduler/runtime authority. Historical `packet_wake_attempted` / `reviewer_wake` rows remain audit evidence, not current permission to spawn conductors.
      progress: 2026-05-01 Claude dashboard packet `rev_pkt_2710` also exposed a live `triage --probe-report` import failure in `dev/scripts/checks/mutation_outcome_parse.py`. The public shim now imports `mutation_ralph_loop.outcome_parse` package-relatively with a direct-script fallback, so package import, direct script execution, `dev/scripts/mutation/cli.py --results-only --json`, and the mutation loop tests complete without `ModuleNotFoundError`.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-G` Promote architectural findings into durable plan ownership before packet TTL.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-C`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-F`, `MP377-P0-T22AM-F`
      scope: Convert review-only architectural findings into durable plan, finding, decision, or explicit-retirement rows before packet expiry. The write-capable path should extend `PacketDebtRemediationPipeline` / `OrchestratorDispatchDriver` so finding packets become typed `WorkerPacket` work for a plan-integrator lane instead of relying on manual chat or live inbox memory.
      acceptance_criteria: No `finding`, `plan_gap_review`, or plan-scoped `decision` packet can expire without either a durable plan/finding owner, a superseding packet id, or an explicit terminal retirement; `/develop` and startup-context name any packet nearing TTL that lacks a durable owner; a guard/probe fails on growth in `expired_after_durable_binding` rows that have no specific owner beyond the generic `PKT-BIND-*` projection.
      progress: 2026-05-02 urgent integration directive `rev_pkt_2816` promoted the packet stack `rev_pkt_2769`, `rev_pkt_2770`, `rev_pkt_2771`, `rev_pkt_2772`, `rev_pkt_2775`, `rev_pkt_2778`, `rev_pkt_2779`, `rev_pkt_2782`, `rev_pkt_2785`, `rev_pkt_2792`, `rev_pkt_2793`, `rev_pkt_2798`, `rev_pkt_2801`, `rev_pkt_2808`, `rev_pkt_2809`, `rev_pkt_2813`, `rev_pkt_2814`, and `rev_pkt_2815` into `MP377-P0-T22AN-G..R`; `rev_pkt_2815` was already clock-expired but remains integrated here through its archived body and `rev_pkt_2816` supersession.
      progress: 2026-05-04 Claude dogfood packet `rev_pkt_2944` proved the expiry side of the same gap: packet creation binding now gives fresh packets generic `PKT-BIND-*` ownership, but archived legacy packets such as `rev_pkt_2863`, `rev_pkt_2879`, `rev_pkt_2880`, and `rev_pkt_2881` still show `clock_expired_without_disposition` with substantive operator-authorized architecture in the body. Follow-up typed-state probing found 826 unbound substantive expired packets, including 24 operator-authored packets. The closure must wire pre-expiry intent extraction, startup batch-ingest of recently archived unbound packets, and split lifecycle vocabulary so transport expiry cannot masquerade as content disposition.
      progress: 2026-05-04 read-side dogfood closure made that vocabulary split visible for current plan-bound traffic: `PacketLifecycleHistory`, `PacketDisposition`, and `PacketOutcomeLedger` now classify expired rows with `PacketCreationBinding` or durable-ingestion receipts as `expired_after_durable_binding`. The same guard pass promoted missing broad-exception rationales into explicit `allow reason` / `fallback` comments so the default guard floor can block future silent suppressors without forcing unrelated manual archaeology.

- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-V` Make `AgentAttentionLoop` the unified attention decision contract.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`

      scope: Extend the existing `AgentLoopDecision`, wake, packet-continuity, dispatch-router, workstream, safe-auto-apply, action-result, `/develop`, `agent-mind`, `system-map`, and `system-picture` primitives into one shared attention contract consumed by Codex, Claude, and specialist lenses. Inputs include role/lens, open packets, peer state, subprocess outcomes, plan row changes, watchdog signals, role-flip state, route selections, projection freshness, and graph/system-picture signals. Outputs use one typed decision shape with `shape`, `next_action`, `next_pivot_event`, `target_actor`, `target_ref`, route ids, and receipt evidence.

      scope: Add the P0.0 gate that no agent-authored plan is complete until the plan source from Codex, Claude, `/develop`, packet review, temp file, or chat becomes a typed `PlanRow`, typed `PlanProposal`, or explicit rejected/duplicate/obsolete typed receipt. Keep packet text, chat text, and temp files as evidence refs on typed rows or receipts, not as authority.
      acceptance_criteria: A bounded plan-ingestion command/action writes or upserts rows through the repo-pack `MasterPlan` store, emits durable ingestion receipts for terminal outcomes, lets `plan_row_change` and `packet_arrival` attention paths trigger ingestion before TTL/context loss, and fails closed or warns loudly when plan-scoped packets lack durable owner evidence.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-X` Compose packet-aware `/develop`, collaboration modes, and role presets.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-F`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-G`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-M`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-V`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-W`
      scope: Keep `/develop` as the main developer UX over `DevelopmentModeTopology`, `DevelopmentLoopReport`, `DevelopmentScalingContract`, `AgentLoopDecision`, `AuthoritySnapshot`, `SessionPosture`, `CoordinationSnapshot`, and `AgentDispatchRouter`; do not create a parallel scheduler, packet drain loop, or provider-specific role system. Add `CollaborationModeTopology` as a read model only; add `PacketBacklogPressure`, `PacketIntentClassification`, and `PacketAttentionIngestionDecision`; make repo-pack pressure policy configurable with defaults `soft_attention_budget=12`, `hard_attention_budget=15`, and `near_ttl_minutes=10`; add `develop ingest-intent` as the smart packet/chat/file-to-state action while keeping `audit-packets --drain-packets` as compatibility wording. Surface packet pressure, selected classifications, ingestion decisions, role presets, and collaboration mode in `DevelopmentLoopReport`; final-response and continuation guards block handoff when agent-authored durable intent lacks owner or receipt. Add read-only/mutation-filtered role presets for dashboard, implementer, reviewer, architect, researcher, intake, tester, watcher, and operator, plus thin slash adapters that call `/develop` or `agent-loop` with typed role/mode request fields and no embedded policy, provider defaults, or VoiceTerm-specific path authority.
      acceptance_criteria: Packet-pressure tests cover below-budget communication, soft-budget pivot, hard-budget fail-closed, near-TTL durable intent, expired unresolved packets, duplicate/obsolete packets, and communication-only packets; ingestion tests prove every selected durable-intent packet receives exactly one durable owner or terminal receipt; `/develop status`, `next`, `watch`, and `ingest-intent` consume the same attention and packet-pressure state; role preset tests prove slash adapters compile to one backend request model and read-only presets are mutation-filtered; mutable fanout modes require `safe_to_fanout=true`, disjoint scopes, registered worktrees, `OrphanSnapshot` clearance, and leases; portability tests run against an alternate repo root with arbitrary provider ids and repo-pack plan-store paths; dogfood tests run Codex/Claude through `pair_review`, `dashboard_led`, and `intake_fanout` with matching typed row ids, packet classifications, and receipts.
      acceptance_extension: Auto-invocation parity: any packet arrival in any agent inbox triggers an `AgentAttentionLoop` `attention_decision` with shape `packet_arrival` inside the typed wake window, and `PacketIntentClassification` plus `PacketAttentionIngestionDecision` consume that event automatically without requiring a manual `/develop status` invocation.
      acceptance_extension: Auto-invocation parity for plan-intent ingestion: packet kinds carrying durable intent, including `finding`, `plan_patch_review`, and `plan_gap_review`, must reach `PlanIntentIngestion:packet` and emit a `PlanIntentIngestionReceipt` inside the typed wake window; the lighter `PacketCreationBinding` on packet post is necessary provenance but not sufficient durable ingestion closure.
      acceptance_extension: Producer-wake and receipt-field parity: packet wake receipts must be recorded for both Codex-to-Claude and Claude-to-Codex packet arrivals, and every `PlanIntentIngestionReceipt` writer must populate `packet_id` when `source_kind=packet`.
      acceptance_extension: Scoped-guard execution proof: the T22AN-X commit/push path must invoke `check-router --since-ref <base> --execute --dry-run` or an equivalent `ScopedGuardPlan` primitive before broad guard execution, record the changed-path set, routed bundles, skipped empty-intersection guards, and routed-vs-full wall-time delta in a `RunRecord`, and avoid running guard bundles with zero changed-path intersection.
      acceptance_extension: Dashboard subscriber parity: `/dashboard` consumes the same `AgentAttentionLoop` event subscription as the other role adapters; dashboard render/pivot cadence is determined by typed events such as `packet_arrival`, `peer_state_change`, `subprocess_exit`, `plan_row_change`, `watchdog_signal`, and `role_flip`, never by an independent timer or tail-F poll.
      acceptance_extension: File-source plan ingestion and startup-blocked wake visibility: `develop ingest-intent --source <file-path>` must ingest markdown plan files directly as `source_kind=markdown_plan_file` with `PlanIntentIngestionReceipt` coverage, and packet-arrival wake receipts must emit `attention_decision_shape=startup_blocked`, the startup-authority blocker, `safe`, `may_mutate`, and the closure check command when typed `AgentLoopDecision` shows startup authority blocks the wake.
      acceptance_extension: Session-start bootstrap and bidirectional wake closure: new Codex and Claude sessions must prove the typed bootstrap trio (`startup-context`, `session-resume`, and `review-channel --action inbox/status`) before acting on chat handoff, while Codex, Claude, dashboard, and watcher lanes share the same `AgentAttentionLoop` subscription for packet arrivals, plan-row changes, peer-state changes, subprocess exits, watchdog signals, and role flips. A session-start guard must fail closed or emit an explicit typed override receipt before any non-bootstrap execution when that proof is missing.
      acceptance_extension: Actor-route authority is platform-wide: packets, slash adapters, `/develop`, smart-router routes, startup/session-resume, dashboards, and commit/push gates must treat provider ids (`codex`, `claude`, `cursor`, etc.) as delivery adapters only. Authority routes through actor role, exact session id when a live session is intended, and required capability grants from `CollaborationSession` / `AuthoritySnapshot`. Compatibility fields such as `from_agent` / `to_agent` may remain for transport, but they must not decide reviewer, implementer, architect, watcher, dashboard, or operator authority by themselves.
      acceptance_extension: Exact-session wake binding: provider-targeted packets must not be satisfied by spawning an unbound headless provider process. Delivery may record typed attention without process wake, but an existing target session must observe the packet through typed polling, and a new worker launch requires actor role plus capability evidence. `requested_session_visibility` is role-neutral: `dashboard_only` means typed poll only, `visible` means a user-visible worker launch, and `headless` is suppressed until explicit typed approval/proof marks the route headless-approved. Wake receipts must distinguish `visible_session_woke=true`, `visible_session_woke=false`, `session_poll`, `headless_requires_typed_approval`, and `target_session_binding_required`. A dogfood pass is not accepted until the intended actor/session observes or responds through typed packet state, not merely because a detached process was launched.
      progress: 2026-05-03 first slice started by adding read-only `CollaborationModeTopology` plus `PacketAttentionPressurePolicy` to `/develop`; default collaboration modes are `solo`, `pair_review`, `dashboard_led`, `intake_fanout`, `research_fanout`, `review_fanout`, `watcher_fanout`, `isolated_builder_fanout`, and `dogfood_campaign`; role presets are `dashboard`, `implementer`, `reviewer`, `architect`, `researcher`, `intake`, `tester`, `watcher`, and `operator`. The read model reports selected mode, selected role preset, packet pressure budgets, and mutable-fanout gate status, but authority remains with `AuthoritySnapshot`, `SessionPosture`, `AgentDispatchRouter`, `OrphanSnapshot`, and leases.
      progress: 2026-05-03 second slice added `/develop` packet pressure as typed read models: `PacketBacklogPressure`, `PacketIntentClassification`, and `PacketAttentionIngestionDecision` now report live/actionable counts, near-TTL packets, expired unresolved packets, carry-forward debt, durable-owner gaps, per-kind/per-role pressure, selected classifications, and the attention decision without draining packets. `develop ingest-intent` is wired as the packet/chat/file-to-typed-state alias over `PlanIntentIngestionReceipt`, while `dogfood_campaign` explicitly includes Claude dogfood packet surfaces as evidence-only packet review.
      progress: 2026-05-06 dogfood closed the `audit-packets` continuation self-loop: when packet attention has already routed into the audit reducer, `PacketAttentionIngestionDecision.next_command` now becomes the continuation command, top-level `next_step_command`, and first `next_commands` row. This keeps automation on the selected bounded packet action such as `review-channel --action show --packet-id <id>` instead of requiring another manual audit pass.
      progress: 2026-05-03 Claude dogfood packet `rev_pkt_2830` was ingested into T22AN-X: dashboard role presets now surface `AgentAttentionLoop` subscription parity and `dogfood_campaign` names dashboard subscriber-parity proof as a required dogfood surface.
      progress: 2026-05-03 Claude dogfood packet `rev_pkt_2831` proved the manual-invocation gap: `/develop status` classified `rev_pkt_2829` correctly, but packet arrival still needed a typed auto-invocation edge. `event_post_wake` now records `PacketWakeReceipt` events with `attention_decision_shape=packet_arrival` even when no conductor wake helper returns a process wake report.
      progress: 2026-05-03 Claude dogfood packet `rev_pkt_2832` verified the new producer-wake parity edge by arriving with `PacketWakeReceipt.wake_method=typed_attention_event` and `attention_decision_shape=packet_arrival`; T22AN-X now also records plan-intent auto-invocation and packet-id receipt-field parity as acceptance extensions.
      progress: 2026-05-03 Claude dogfood packet `rev_pkt_2833` folded scoped-guard execution proof into T22AN-X. A `check-router --since-ref origin/develop --execute --dry-run` proof ran, but the current branch range selected the release lane because `origin/develop...HEAD` contains 2727 changed paths including release-sensitive files; this is recorded as evidence that the final commit/push proof needs a narrower current-slice base before broad guards are trusted.
      progress: 2026-05-03 Claude dogfood packet `rev_pkt_2834` was ingested as `PlanIntentIngestionReceipt` `plan-ingest-334b741b6d7f2b15`; `/develop ingest-intent --source` now reads markdown plan files directly, `/develop` labels `actor_pending_packet_count` as inbox-scoped, and packet wake receipts can report `attention_decision_shape=startup_blocked` with `startup_blocker`, `safe`, `may_mutate`, and `closure_check_command` when startup authority such as `import_index_atomicity` is blocking the wake.
      progress: 2026-05-03 Claude dogfood packets `rev_pkt_2835` and `rev_pkt_2836` were ingested as `PlanIntentIngestionReceipt` rows `plan-ingest-f77eb6a7b5ff3c7f` and `plan-ingest-88d1e48869d657d1`; T22AN-X now treats typed-blocker wake evidence, the shared Codex/Claude role-adapter matrix, the generated provider-neutral role catalog, and the session-start bootstrap bypass as in-scope closure work instead of chat-only feedback.
      progress: 2026-05-03 scoped-router dogfood now uses the live worktree path set for this uncommitted slice. The first dry-run selected `bundle.tooling` but falsely attached Rust ANSI parser long tests because `dev/scripts/devctl/commands/development/parser.py` matched the broad `/parser` token; the risk add-on policy was narrowed to `rust/src/parser`, and the rerun selected the same tooling lane with `risk_addons=[]`.
      progress: 2026-05-03 physical Codex/Claude wake dogfood found that packet `rev_pkt_2839` launched a detached/headless Claude conductor instead of the visible dashboard Claude session (`554f3c92-4b98-4c75-a415-e125fe6ef248`). This is a blocking design smell, not wake success: the typed registry collapsed distinct provider sessions and `woke=true` only proved process spawn. T22AN-X is now being corrected as a platform-wide actor-route invariant: unbound provider packet wakes fail closed with `reason=target_session_binding_required`, existing target sessions use `wake_method=session_poll`, new workers default to `requested_session_visibility=visible` plus capability-gated launch, and `headless` is blocked until typed approval/proof exists.
      progress: 2026-05-03 smart-router dogfood packet `rev_pkt_2838` was verified against command output. `bundle.tooling` now includes `check_function_duplication.py`, `check-router --execute --dry-run --format json` serializes dry-run steps without leaking text before JSON, and `check-router --since-ref <base>` uses dirty worktree paths as the current slice when uncommitted work exists instead of routing a long-lived branch range such as `origin/develop...HEAD`.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-Y` Consolidate duplicated governance, wake, router, and typed-next surfaces.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `queued`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-V`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-W`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-X`
      scope: Use `rev_pkt_2840` as the typed evidence packet for the consolidation lane. Close duplicated or mirrored surfaces before adding new ones: retire review-channel state/projection mirror writes once all readers resolve through the projection locator, import one wake-path resolver and one packet actor-route predicate, keep provider ids as transport-only fields, route smart-router validation from the current slice path set, and move typed-next fields that currently only render display text behind a shared executor/gate.
      acceptance_criteria: Wave 1 deletes or deprecates the duplicate `state/latest.json` and `latest/*` review-channel mirror writers after reader coverage is proven, removes duplicate wake-path and actor-route helpers, wires exact-session wake receipts to `session_poll` without headless spawn, and keeps `check_function_duplication.py` in the routed tooling bundle. Wave 2 collapses overlapping surface-sync, platform-contract, and review-snapshot guards into parameterized shared guards, stops persisting derived mode dimensions that can be computed from typed topology/authority, and narrows `/develop` contract rows to canonical state plus projections. Wave 3 extracts fat slash-command decision logic into typed runtime commands and introduces one capability-gated typed-next executor for emitted `recommended_command`, `next_command`, `recovery_action_allowed`, and wait-recovery fields. Dogfood proof must include Codex and the visible Claude dashboard observing the same typed row/packet state, not a detached process launch.
      progress: 2026-05-03 Claude packet `rev_pkt_2840` was acknowledged and ingested into typed plan authority as `PlanIntentIngestionReceipt` row `MP377-P0-T22AN-Y`; the first overlap with T22AN-X landed the shared actor-route predicate, reused the canonical wake-path resolver, and updated wake receipts/tests so exact-session packet delivery records `wake_method=session_poll` instead of spawning a headless provider process.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-Z` Add LLM-failure-mode guard and probe coverage.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `queued`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-Q`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-Y`
      scope: Use `rev_pkt_2841` as the typed research/evidence packet for LLM-specific guard expansion. Keep the work inside the existing guard/probe framework and check-router bundle routing rather than adding a parallel quality system. Prioritize concrete, low-false-positive signatures first: phantom imports, naive datetimes, silent exception swallowing, and mutable-default tightening; then add diff-scoped secret/CWE/test-weakening checks; keep higher-noise semantic refactor, docstring-vs-AST, and async-sync checks as probes until false-positive behavior is measured.
      acceptance_criteria: Wave 1 adds `check_phantom_imports.py`, `check_naive_datetime.py`, strict silent-except coverage, and strict mutable-default coverage with focused tests, script-catalog rows, bundle/tooling routing, and docs. Wave 2 adds diff-scoped secret-shape, test-weakening, and LLM-prevalent CWE checks with explicit acknowledgment/ledger semantics where human override is required. Wave 3 adds advisory probes for refactor semantic preservation, docstring claim-vs-AST drift, and async/sync mixing. Every new guard/probe must be routeable through `check-router`, must reuse the existing Python AST/report helpers where possible, and must prove it catches an injected fixture without broad full-repo false positives.
      progress: 2026-05-03 Claude packet `rev_pkt_2841` was acknowledged and ingested into typed plan authority as `PlanIntentIngestionReceipt` row `MP377-P0-T22AN-Z`; its wake receipt used `wake_method=session_poll` for the existing Codex session, so the research was consumed from typed packet state instead of a chat-only or detached-process path.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-AA` Tighten Python quality guards and standard-tool enforcement.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `queued`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-Y`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-Z`
      scope: Use `rev_pkt_2842` as the typed evidence packet for general Python-practices tightening. Prefer existing tool/config ratchets before custom guard growth: expand Ruff's selected rule families, promote bounded mypy enforcement, add commit-time mypy/bandit coverage, wire Python dependency and test-isolation tools, and add only the missing custom guards/probes that cannot be covered by standard tools.
      acceptance_criteria: Wave 1 enables the selected Ruff rule families, promotes mypy enforcement by scoped package with a public-signature `Any` budget, and wires mypy/bandit into pre-commit without creating a separate lint system. Wave 2 adds pip-audit, pytest-randomly, and pytest-timeout through the existing security/test surfaces. Wave 3 adds assertion-less-test, environment-subscript, logger-name, and test-sleep guards with fixture proof, script-catalog rows, bundle/workflow routing, and docs. Wave 4 keeps primitive-obsession, unbounded-queue, and typed-contract property-test suggestions advisory until false-positive behavior is measured.
      progress: 2026-05-03 Claude packet `rev_pkt_2842` was acknowledged and ingested into typed plan authority as `PlanIntentIngestionReceipt` row `MP377-P0-T22AN-AA`; its wake receipt used `wake_method=session_poll` for the existing Codex session and recorded review-only delivery without spawning a provider process.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-AB` Bound Python test execution through typed validation policy.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-AA`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-X`
      scope: Turn the pytest-runaway dogfood failure into a repo-owned validation adapter instead of letting agents and bundles call broad raw pytest directly. Keep the contract portable by treating pytest as this repo's adapter implementation, not universal platform authority.
      acceptance_criteria: Root pytest discovery is scoped and fail-fast with session/per-test timeouts; `devctl test-python` owns named suites and explicit path targets; `check_pytest_runtime_policy.py` blocks raw pytest commands in canonical bundles and missing pytest config policy; `check-router` selects Python test add-ons from touched paths so devctl changes get focused devctl tests and Operator Console tests run only for Python source/test changes; script catalog, bundles, workflows, generated surfaces, and maintainer docs carry the same contract.
      progress: 2026-05-03 first implementation adds root `pytest.ini` / `conftest.py` timeout rails, `devctl test-python`, `check_pytest_runtime_policy.py`, path-aware Python test add-ons in `check-router`, bundle/workflow routing, and focused contract tests. 2026-05-04 governed-push dogfood found that the focused devctl add-on still ran as one quiet 600s pytest command; `test-python` now shards explicit multi-path runs with `--parallel-workers`, and work-intake pacing refuses to live-build the canonical repo graph for temp/external repos that lack saved graph snapshots. 2026-05-06 push preflight first measured `dev/scripts/devctl/tests/commands/test_development_command.py` passing at 314s while the command timed out at 300s, then measured the same focused proof competing with another devctl shard and timing out at 360s after 28 tests, then measured the combined sequential pytest session timing out at 420s after 45 tests. The focused devctl add-on now splits selected targets into serial single-target sessions with a 420s measured floor, target-specific measured overrides such as 600s for `test_development_command.py`, and a bounded per-test cap. 2026-05-08 push-preflight dogfood then measured `dev/scripts/devctl/tests/vcs/test_push.py` passing 61 tests before the 600s session timeout; the unit tests now mock live repo-scale projection scans, `check-router` routes that file as class-level pytest node-id shards with four `test-python` workers, and the typed target floor is reduced to 240s after measured 80.48s serial / 48.31s sharded proof.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-PIPELINE-SCOPE-VALIDATION-S1` Thread typed validation scope through governed publication checks.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-AB`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-AE`
      scope: Governed publication needs to validate the authorized commit range without letting unrelated live worktree dirt or projection-liveness drift masquerade as proof that the committed range is invalid. Add a typed `ValidationScope` / `ValidationContext` contract shared by `check-router`, `docs-check`, push preflight command construction, and live projection guards. Keep standalone checks fail-closed under the default `live_worktree` scope; use `pipeline_authorized_phase` only from governed publication so affected live guards still run, preserve their original `live_worktree_ok` and errors as evidence, and report advisory nonblocking status for the publication phase.
      acceptance_criteria: `devctl push --execute` routes preflight through `check-router --validation-scope pipeline_authorized_phase`; `check-router` keeps docs-check and live projection guards planned instead of hiding them in a skip list; docs-check passes the same scope into nested strict-tooling subgates; tandem, bridge, surface, multi-agent, startup-authority, and system-picture live guards accept the scope and annotate any advisory pass with original live errors; standalone guard and docs-check invocations remain strict by default; generated boot cards, maintainer docs, plan docs, and evolution notes carry the same contract; focused router/docs/push tests and live guard CLI probes cover the behavior.
      progress: 2026-05-09 packets `rev_pkt_3329`, `rev_pkt_3334`, `rev_pkt_3343`, `rev_pkt_3344`, `rev_pkt_3345`, `rev_pkt_3346`, `rev_pkt_3347`, `rev_pkt_3349`, `rev_pkt_3350`, `rev_pkt_3356`, and Claude architecture approval `rev_pkt_3357` converged the design: live guards remain visible evidence, but governed push may publish an already-authorized commit range while unrelated live-worktree state remains advisory. The implementation adds the shared validation-scope runtime contract, threads it through push preflight, router, docs-check, and live guard CLIs, and records the ground-truth receipt for the new validation path. 2026-05-11 push dogfood tightened the same row: publication preflight now fails fast by default through repo policy `fail_fast_on_blocker=true`, the in-flight push report says no git push has started until validation passes, and external ReviewSnapshot receipt commits no longer trigger another external ReviewSnapshot receipt commit.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-AC` Automate fresh-session typed orientation.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-X`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-Y`
      scope: Replace ad hoc "where are we / next steps" reconstruction with one governed `devctl session` orientation command. The command must run `startup-context`, `session-resume`, `review-channel --action status --terminal none`, and `context-graph --mode bootstrap` in order, preserve startup blockers as typed evidence, keep context-graph snapshot writes enabled for freshness, and reduce the preferred `AuthoritySnapshot` into the next command.
      acceptance_criteria: `devctl session --role <role> --format md|json` emits `SessionOrientationPacket`; startup-context non-zero typed blockers do not stop session-resume/status/context-graph reads; reviewer/implementer/observer roles are supported; docs/bootstrap surfaces tell fresh sessions to use `devctl session` before answering; focused tests cover command order, review-status inclusion, artifact suppression override, and dashboard-to-observer normalization.
      progress: 2026-05-03 operator dogfood found a fresh-session bootstrap miss where a new Codex session inferred state from git/docs without typed startup, review-status, or context-graph reads; that instruction was ingested into typed plan authority as `MP377-P0-T22AN-AC`. 2026-05-08 dogfood tightened the live orientation reducer and generated boot cards: `devctl session` is now the first generated bootstrap command, and `SessionOrientationPacket.final.next_command` no longer promotes startup `run_devctl_push` when the preferred review/status `AuthoritySnapshot` says `safe_to_continue=false` or blocks `vcs.push`.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-AD` Align slash entry points to typed governance domains.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `queued`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-X`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-Y`
      scope: Use archived Claude packet `rev_pkt_2863` as the durable architecture input for the "slash = typed domain" catalog. Treat the packet as a planning/domain-catalog slice, not an immediate removal of existing `/develop --role-preset reviewer|tester|intake|watcher` compatibility presets. Canonical domains should route `/develop` to development/mutation orchestration, `/review` to packet/finding lifecycle, `/test` to test evidence, `/intake` or `/plan` to typed plan/finding/guard ingestion, `/watch` to runtime observation, `/system` or `/status` to startup/control-plane state, and a slimmer `/bridge-loop` to launch/control.
      acceptance_criteria: A typed slash-domain catalog or equivalent read model maps each slash to one backend domain without embedding policy in provider slash files; existing `/develop` role presets remain compatibility aliases until replacement slashes have typed contracts, docs, and tests; `.claude/commands/*` and generated slash templates stay thin adapters; guards/tests prove every slash maps to typed backend commands and no provider file becomes authority.
      progress: 2026-05-03 Claude packet `rev_pkt_2863` expired before disposition but was read from typed packet history and ingested into durable plan authority as `PlanIntentIngestionReceipt` row `MP377-P0-T22AN-AD`; no immediate preset removal is authorized by this row.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-AE` Automate stage-handoff-to-publication convergence.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-H`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-I`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-J`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-O`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-X`
      scope: Replace the manual `stage_commit_pipeline` packet -> ACK/apply -> startup-context -> projection-drift absorb -> routed guard proof -> governed push retry -> packet evidence closeout sequence with one typed handoff-to-publication controller. The controller should consume `AgentSessionOutcome(outcome=completed_handoff)`, safe-auto-apply `action_request` packets, `CheckRouterGuardCoverageReceipt`, `GuardRemediationAction`, `PushEnforcement`, `PipelineAutoRecoveryReceipt`, managed-receipt classification, publisher/VCS coordination locks, and current `AgentLoopDecision` state as inputs. It should freeze the handoff target revision/worktree identity, auto-ACK/apply only packets with runtime authority evidence, repair managed projection drift through receipt-only paths, iterate managed receipt/projection refresh to a bounded fixed point, wait instead of racing when source paths or HEAD move outside the managed receipt chain, run `check-router --execute --keep-going` and then `devctl push --execute` when typed startup says `run_devctl_push`, and emit one durable `StageHandoffPublicationRun` / `ActionResult` trail that closes the packet lifecycle.
      acceptance_criteria: The live `rev_pkt_2929` dogfood transcript is captured as a regression fixture with phases for clean handoff, managed projection drift, bridge-triggered receipt cascade, concurrent source edits, routed guard validation with all planned commands either executed or typed-skipped, push validation recovery, and final packet application. A single controller command or `agent-loop --execute` tick can advance each deterministic phase without chat-authored shell decisions; concurrent source edits yield `wait_for_new_completed_handoff` rather than another push attempt; managed projection receipts converge within a bounded attempt count without hand-authored drift commits; publisher refresh cannot write during the VCS critical section except through typed receipt state; output reports list valid next actions, executed actions, blocked actions, source-vs-managed HEAD movement, guard coverage/remediation receipts, and exact evidence refs.
      progress: 2026-05-03 operator dogfood around `rev_pkt_2929` proved the missing end-to-end controller: Codex produced a safe-auto-apply `stage_commit_pipeline` handoff, but the watcher still had to manually ACK, classify startup push state, absorb projection drift, retry governed push, detect post-commit receipt cascades, and stop when Codex resumed source edits. 2026-05-04 follow-up proved the guard-evidence side of the same gap: c2cf00e6 had no typed proof that all required guards ran, so this slice promoted check-router coverage/remediation receipts, governed push preflight `--keep-going`, and the shared cross-cutting guard floor as controller prerequisites. Claude packet `rev_pkt_2946` then caught the self-hosting performance gap in the same lane: routed `--keep-going` execution must use the existing parallel check-step runner instead of serially shelling every independent command. The next dogfood loop caught the adjacent silent-progress gap: governed commit now streams VCS phase progress through the irreversible `git commit` subprocess and announces the post-commit ReviewSnapshot receipt hook so the publication controller has observable phase boundaries while it advances toward push. The routed-guard dogfood immediately after that exposed the missing timeout half of the same design: serial-required guards such as startup-authority may run alone by design, but they now carry command and route budgets and fail as typed guard evidence instead of holding the route silently.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-AF` Separate packet attention from conductor launch authority.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-X`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-Y`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-AE`
      scope: Packets are communication, plan-intake, and typed attention input only. Packet post, packet delivery, follow-loop attention, inbox/watch, and packet-backed control paths must never launch, replace, clean up, or externally wake Codex, Claude, Cursor, or future provider sessions. Session starts, rollover, replacement, and worker fanout belong to scheduler/runtime controllers after explicit task boundaries such as `AgentSessionOutcome(outcome=completed_handoff)` plus typed plan/workboard authority. Provider ids remain transport fields; actor role, exact session id, and capability grants decide who may consume or execute packet intent.
      acceptance_criteria: Event-backed packet post and follow-loop attention return an explicit no-launch attention receipt (`attempted=false`, `woke=false`, `wake_method=none`, `attention_recorded=true`) while preserving the packet in typed queue/attention state; tests prove packet attention does not call launcher, cleanup, headless delegate, or relaunch dependencies; markdown renders no-launch delivery as "Packet Attention" rather than process wake success; post JSON exposes `packet_attention` as the primary receipt with `reviewer_wake` only as a compatibility alias; `single_agent`/inactive projections do not promote fresh Claude packet activity into detached runtime liveness; docs state that packets can feed plans and scheduler inputs but cannot start a conductor.
      progress: 2026-05-04 operator dogfood caught the core architecture mistake: a packet path still implied that delivery could wake or spawn conductors. This row keeps the immediate code/test/docs fix and the broader scheduler boundary in typed plan state instead of leaving it in chat or transient packet prose. The first patch turns event-post and follow-controller delivery into no-launch typed attention receipts and guards that launch dependencies are not called. Claude dogfood packet `rev_pkt_2940` then verified the live no-launch behavior for `rev_pkt_2938`/`rev_pkt_2939` and closed two reporting follow-ups: event-backed post reports now prefer `packet_attention`, and communication-only `system_notice` receipts with plan anchors report advisory plan context instead of implying no context existed. `/develop` peer-mind output now renders `attention_hint` / `attention_reason` and includes concrete poll commands in `next_commands` so cross-agent packet attention is surfaced without creating a wake/spawn path.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-AG` Enforce cold-session typed bootstrap chain.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `queued`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-AC`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-AE`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-AF`
      scope: Use `rev_pkt_2936` Class F as the durable architecture input for a `BootstrapReactor` or equivalent gate. A cold session must prove `startup-context`, role-bound `session-resume`, and `context-graph --mode bootstrap` receipts before non-bootstrap tool calls or work selection. `session-resume` may orchestrate the chain, but the runtime must enforce the receipts instead of relying on CLAUDE.md/Codex prose convention.
      acceptance_criteria: A session-scoped bootstrap receipt records command order, payload digests, role/session binding, context-graph snapshot path, and freshness; non-bootstrap tool calls fail closed with `bootstrap_incomplete` until the receipts are current; startup blockers halt mutation but still preserve orientation evidence; focused tests cover missing step 0, missing role resume, stale context graph, startup non-ok, and a green fresh-session path; dogfood proves a fresh Claude/Codex session cannot skip typed bootstrap and still claim valid guard/push work.
      progress: 2026-05-04 Claude packet `rev_pkt_2936` was read from packet history and accepted as the Class F follow-up to `rev_pkt_2932`: typed bootstrap surfaces exist and work, but consumption is voluntary. This row makes the enforcement slice explicit and keeps it separate from the immediate packet-no-launch hotfix.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-AH` Promote deterministic semantic-guard contracts.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `queued`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-D`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-AF`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-Q`
      scope: Turn Claude packet `rev_pkt_2942` into deterministic semantic guard coverage so AI-generated vocabulary drift and type re-coining are caught by the system instead of by operator grep. The first guard slice should extend the existing `probe_term_consistency` / `check_contract_connectivity` foundations into typed hardening records for contract renames, vocabulary contracts, and dataclass shape clustering. The initial records are `wake_to_attention_2026_05_03`, the `/develop` next-command vocabulary split, and Session*/Packet*/Receipt* dataclass clusters.
      acceptance_criteria: Typed `ContractRenameRecord` / `VocabularyContract` / clustering-exception records exist in repo-owned config or code; deterministic checks scan source without LLM calls; soft vs hard deadlines are explicit; scheduler-legitimate `wake_*` paths stay allowed while packet-attention old vocabulary is flagged; dataclass clustering reports cluster id, root term, files, field overlap, and exception rationale; check-router guard coverage can report these commands through `CheckRouterGuardCoverageReceipt` / `GuardRemediationAction` before promotion into the shared floor.
      progress: 2026-05-04 Claude dogfood packet `rev_pkt_2942` captured the operator's "deterministic but semantic" ask and tied it to the wake-to-attention rename, next-command vocabulary drift, and Session* dataclass proliferation. This row keeps the implementation as a guard-promotion slice instead of allowing the packet body to remain transient design prose.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-AI` Route architectural findings into bounded AI working memory.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `queued`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-F`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-G`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-AH`
      scope: Turn Claude packet `rev_pkt_2943` into the routing layer that makes existing governance/probe/guard findings actionable instead of leaving them in large reports. The first slice should introduce an `ArchitecturalSuggestionPacket` or equivalent typed routing row that aggregates `governance-quality-feedback`, `governance-review`, `probe_packet_carry_forward_debt`, and check-router guard coverage; filters by current touched files or active slice; ranks by severity, recency, and confidence; and surfaces at most three active suggestions per actor through `/develop` next-command attention.
      acceptance_criteria: `/develop` and startup/bootstrap surfaces can show top-N architectural suggestions without launching agents or creating unbounded packet pressure; stale suggestions either gain durable plan/finding ownership or escalate to operator review after a bounded age; the routing path consumes existing typed probe payloads such as `required_next_action` / AI instruction text before inventing new schemas; slash and graph/catalog entry points remain thin adapters over the same typed control plane; tests prove the suggestions are read-side attention until an explicit plan-ingestion/write command is invoked.
      progress: 2026-05-04 Claude packet `rev_pkt_2943` synthesized the operator's 0%-cleanup dogfood evidence: mechanical findings are fixed, while architectural-judgment findings remain ignored unless routed into the AI working surface. This row keeps the high-leverage routing work separate from the guard-authoring work in `MP377-P0-T22AN-AH` and the packet-expiry ownership work in `MP377-P0-T22AN-G`.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-AJ` Build scheduler-owned cross-AI relaunch-loop dispatch.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-AF`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-L`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-X`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-Y`
      scope: Turn Claude packet `rev_pkt_2975` into the portable relaunch-loop layer that prevents Codex/Claude wait-deadlock between slices without giving packet delivery process authority. A closed bounded slice emits `SliceClosureEvent`; a scheduler-owned watcher converts cross-actor next-slice targets into idempotent `AgentRelaunchTrigger` rows; quota receipts fail closed on runaway loops; a dispatcher may only spawn after typed provider/conductor registry authority proves no duplicate live session and the launch command is capability-scoped.
      acceptance_criteria: The first slice ships typed contracts plus `devctl relaunch-loop --action emit-closure|watch-once|dispatch-once --dry-run`; dogfood proves a Codex-to-Claude closure becomes one queued trigger and a dry-run launch preview, with duplicate watch ticks skipped; provider spawning remains fail-closed until registry/authority checks land; later slices wire automatic closure emission from governed push, reviewer checkpoint, and packet lifecycle terminal states, then add the actual devctl daemon and optional launchd/systemd wrapper.
      progress: 2026-05-04 Codex reviewed `rev_pkt_2975` and posted decision packet `rev_pkt_2978`: choose a devctl-owned controller before OS service deployment, keep packet delivery as attention only, use bounded typed session seeds, enforce per-actor/shared quotas, prefer local repo launch adapters before Anthropic Routine fire, and require provider/conductor registry checks before any spawn. The first implementation adds `SliceClosureEvent`, `AgentRelaunchTrigger`, `RelaunchQuotaExceeded`, platform contract rows, focused tests, and live dogfood state under `dev/state/relaunch_loop/` proving trace -> queue -> dry-run dispatch for a Claude target.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-AK` Calibrate design-quality guards against shallow syntactic appeasement.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `queued`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-AH`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-AI`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-AJ`
      scope: Turn the relaunch-loop dogfood findings into a guard-quality calibration slice: code-shape, parameter-count, dict-schema, and facade-wrapper checks should push agents toward typed contracts, route tables, validating parsers, and model/builder/store separation, not toward mutable-global registry rewrites or no-op temporary variables that only satisfy an AST pattern. The first calibration corpus is the relaunch-loop split, including `COMMAND_HANDLERS`, `SliceClosureInput` / `RelaunchTriggerInput`, and `from_mapping` constructors that perform coercion/normalization.
      acceptance_criteria: Guard fixtures distinguish intentional typed command registries from untyped large dict literals; facade-wrapper detection exempts constructors/parsers only when they prove coercion, validation, normalization, or fail-closed rejection rather than pure pass-through; parameter-count remediation is accepted only when a named input contract replaces raw long argument lists without becoming an unvalidated catch-all bag; check-router / docs output explains the design property being enforced; regression tests include both the good relaunch-loop split and shallow appeasement counterexamples.
      progress: 2026-05-04 operator review of the relaunch-loop guard failure found that the module split and typed input contracts were genuine architectural improvements, while the temporary `COMMAND_HANDLERS` assignment style and `target = cls(...); return target` facade workaround were shallow guard appeasement. This row keeps that learning in durable plan authority and routes it to Claude/Codex implementation through typed review-channel packets instead of memory-only advice.
- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-AM` Add continuous peer attention windows with blocker-only mutation interruption.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `queued`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-AF`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-AI`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-X`
      disposition_sources: `rev_pkt_3042`, `rev_pkt_3043`, `rev_pkt_3044`
      scope: Add a typed `PacketUrgency`, `PacketAttentionPolicy`, per-actor `AttentionWindow`, `PeerPacketRow`, packet-body observation receipt, and in-flight attention signal model so reviewer, architect, operator, and system packets become continuous ambient context for active implementers instead of requiring a human paste or a reactive stop. Packet posts recompute relevance for known peer actors using active plan row, dirty paths, role/session, packet kind/severity, target fields, and current slice; body-bearing peer packets require explicit `packet_body_observed` evidence before mutation can continue; high-relevance urgent packets enter the peer attention window as ambient context for the next turn, while only truly blocking urgency or mutation-revoking packets enter `blocking_consume_required` and feed `AgentStopGate` / `AuthorityContinuationDecision` before the next mutation.
      acceptance_criteria: A new P0/P1 finding packet for the active plan row appears in the target actor's `AttentionWindow.peer_recent_packets` with relevance score, consume status, and exact `review-channel show --actor <actor>` command; `review-channel --action show --packet-id <id> --actor <actor>` emits `PacketBodyObservation` / `packet_body_observed` evidence with actor, packet id, source event id, body digest, and observation time; agent-loop and `/develop next` block mutation with `open_packet_body` for unread body-bearing peer packets; in-flight packet/finding composition can publish a predicted inbound signal so peers know review feedback is likely soon without stopping; a blocking packet sets `may_mutate=false` and `required_action=consume_packet`; non-blocking urgent reviewer feedback is visible in agent-loop, `/develop`, dashboard, and review-channel status without stopping normal in-scope edits after body observation; packet attention remains typed state only and does not launch or replace provider sessions.
      progress: 2026-05-11 Codex folded smell #057 into this owner row: inbox/watch metadata no longer counts as packet-body consumption. The implementation adds `PacketBodyObservation` events from `review-channel show --actor`, projects body observation fields onto packet rows, preserves them through fast pending-queue readers, status prior-state merge, typed `ReviewPacketState`, canonical projection bundles, compact agent-loop output, and watch signatures, threads body-open requirements through `PacketAttentionState`, blocks agent-loop mutation with `open_packet_body`, makes `/develop next` issue the body-open show command for Claude-to-Codex progress/finding packets, and keeps watcher launch/auto-wake follow-up under this row instead of minting a parallel `MP377-PEER-PACKET-INGESTION-S1` authority. Codex posted `rev_pkt_3682` to Claude with the concrete verification/audit asks; Claude answered in `rev_pkt_3683` with 3/4 surfaces verified and reviewer-cycle discipline receipt left as a distinct follow-up. Codex backfilled the session-2 Claude packets through the real `show --actor codex` path, drained the remaining required body queue through `rev_pkt_3615`, and current live proof reports `body_open_required=false`, `unopened_body_packet_count=0`, and `continue_to_goal` on `rev_pkt_3630` instead of silent implementation against metadata-only packet evidence.

Preserve those fields through packet lifecycle, inbox, attention, plan ingestion, and relevance classification.

- [ ] `MP377-P0-T22AN-AO` Route architecture/proof-channel design through ground-truth preflight.
      phase_id: `MP377-P0`
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `queued`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T22AN-C`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-M`, `MP377-P0-T22AN-AN`

      scope: Add a connected design preflight so architecture, runtime-state, and proof-channel work starts from upstream/repo truth instead of designing sidecar contracts first. The pipeline runs `/develop design-preflight`, reduces current review/runtime state into `RuntimeTruthSnapshot`, probes agent-mind, provider-owned session state such as Claude `~/.claude/sessions/<pid>.json` `bridgeSessionId`, connectivity, command registry, startup quality signals, and existing contracts, then records `GroundTruthProbeRunReceipt` before implementation proceeds.
      acceptance_criteria: Runtime/proof/architecture edits that touch trigger paths fail `check_ground_truth_probe_gate.py` unless a current satisfied `GroundTruthProbeRunReceipt` covers the changed paths digest and required probes; `/develop design-preflight --topic "<state/proof topic>"` reports whether to reuse an existing surface, extend an existing contract, block for missing topic, or justify a new contract; `RuntimeTruthSnapshot` is registered as a reducer over ReviewState/runtime evidence rather than a parallel authority store; new proof-channel design must name upstream state sources or existing contracts before adding typed surfaces; maintainer docs and command registries advertise the pipeline as the architecture-safe entrypoint.

      progress: 2026-05-11 governed mutation self-healing follow-up aligned startup/work-intake push-preflight projections with fail-fast publication semantics: projected push preflight omits `--keep-going` unless policy explicitly requests audit mode, and managed projection receipt cleanup refuses to stack another managed receipt when `HEAD` is already a ReviewSnapshot/generated-surface receipt. Evidence: `dev/scripts/devctl/runtime/work_intake_routing.py`, `dev/scripts/devctl/commands/vcs/push_projection_receipt.py`, `dev/scripts/devctl/tests/runtime/test_work_intake.py`, `dev/scripts/devctl/tests/runtime/test_startup_context.py`, and `dev/scripts/devctl/tests/vcs/test_push.py`.
- [ ] `MP377-P1-T08` Emit one typed liveness/wake/death signal family for reviewer, implementer, publisher, and observer consumers so dashboard/mobile/startup/status can distinguish `alive`, `degraded`, `detached_runtime_only`, and `dead` from one producer instead of mixing heartbeat prose, PID guesses, and bridge hints. Compatibility projections like `Claude Ack` and bridge heartbeat sections may mirror that state, but they must not remain independent wake/repair gates once typed runtime evidence exists.
      owner_doc: `dev/active/remote_control_runtime.md`
      status: `blocked`
      depends_on: `MP377-P1-T06`, `MP397-P0`
      progress: 2026-04-28 `dashboard --follow`, `claude-loop`, `mobile-status`, and Operator Console now surface active Codex sessions, session outcomes, ACK freshness, and agent-mind from `DashboardSnapshot` v3; Codex launch watchdogs refresh the agent-mind projection when rollout traces advance. Later 2026-04-28 work moved the `alive` / `degraded` / `detached_runtime_only` / `dead` provider signal into `runtime.session_liveness_signal`, has status emit it as `session_liveness_signals` with `participant_liveness` as compatibility projection, adds a DashboardSnapshot `session_liveness` section, and makes startup/control-plane counts prefer the typed signal instead of direct bridge conductor booleans. Remaining T08 scope is broader wake/death producer parity after the P1-T06 producer-tick collapse lands.
- [x] `MP377-P1-T09` Land slice-one worktree-orphan typed contracts and platform registration for orphan snapshots, checkout inventory, publication ledgers, session leases, baselines, reconciliation decisions, and accept-all receipts.
      owner_doc: `dev/active/ai_governance_platform.md`
      status: `done`
      depends_on: `MP377-P1-T04`

### Phase MP-399 - Governed Commit Staged-Index Preservation

Phase metadata: phase_id=MP399-P0; owner_doc=`dev/active/remote_commit_pipeline.md`; status=in_progress; depends_on=`MP377-P0`; summary=Make `devctl commit` preserve user-staged content through the managed ReviewSnapshot refresh and report the governed content commit separately from any trailing receipt commit.

- [ ] `MP399-T01` Fail closed when the managed ReviewSnapshot refresh drops preexisting user-staged paths from the index instead of silently replacing the staged set with repo-owned artifacts.
      owner_doc: `dev/active/remote_commit_pipeline.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T05`
- [ ] `MP399-T02` Keep governed commit reporting truthful when a trailing ReviewSnapshot receipt commit advances `HEAD`: surface the staged/content commit distinctly from the snapshot-only receipt so operators do not infer staged work was lost.
      owner_doc: `dev/active/remote_commit_pipeline.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP399-T01`
- [ ] `MP399-T03` Add regression coverage proving user-staged content survives governed staging/commit execution and that receipt-aware commit reporting stays explicit.
      owner_doc: `dev/active/remote_commit_pipeline.md`
      status: `in_progress`
      depends_on: `MP399-T02`

2026-04-22 dogfood extension: MP399-T01 now also covers the no-user-staged
variant. Governed staging fails with `staged_scope_missing_dirty_work` when the
managed ReviewSnapshot refresh leaves only receipt artifacts staged while real
dirty work remains outside the index; use an explicit receipt command for
snapshot-only commits.

### Phase MP-412 - Harness Authorization Contract

### Phase MP-413 - Staged Intent And Worker Output Closure

Phase metadata: phase_id=MP413-P0; owner_doc=`dev/active/remote_commit_pipeline.md`; status=pending; depends_on=`MP412-P0`; summary=Close staged-intent and worker-created-file staging gaps so governed mutation preserves user intent without manual `git add` repair.

      status: `pending`
      depends_on: `MP412-P0`

### Phase MP-414 - Typed Decision Policy

Phase metadata: phase_id=MP414-P0; owner_doc=`dev/active/platform_authority_loop.md`; status=pending; depends_on=`MP412-P0`; summary=Promote typed decision policy so A/B/C architecture choices land in one repo-owned decision surface instead of packet prose.

      owner_doc: `dev/active/platform_authority_loop.md`
      status: `pending`
      depends_on: `MP412-P0`
- [ ] `MP414-T02` Promote `rev_pkt_1221`, `rev_pkt_1222`, `rev_pkt_1223`, and `rev_pkt_1234` into typed decision authority, including explicit branch choices for `look-first`, graph/guard expansion, and attention-stale handling before those packets leave the pending queue.
      owner_doc: `dev/active/platform_authority_loop.md`
      status: `pending`
      depends_on: `MP414-P0`

### Phase MP-416 - Reviewer Wake On Packet Post

Phase metadata: phase_id=MP416-P0; owner_doc=`dev/active/review_channel.md`; status=done; depends_on=`MP415-P0`; summary=Close reviewer wake-on-packet-post so repo-owned review traffic can relaunch or notify the reviewer lane without manual polling.

### Phase MP-417 - Snapshot Drift Ordering

Phase metadata: phase_id=MP417-P0; owner_doc=`dev/active/remote_commit_pipeline.md`; status=pending; depends_on=`MP416-P0`; summary=Fix snapshot-drift and governance-refresh ordering so commit/push and review status stop dirtying the worktree after stage approval.

- [ ] `MP417-T01` Move or harden the post-stage refresh path so staged-tree approval, review-state refresh, and compatibility projection writes stay idempotent and do not regenerate dirty worktree churn after approval.
      owner_doc: `dev/active/remote_commit_pipeline.md`
      status: `pending`
      depends_on: `MP416-P0`
- [ ] `MP417-T02` Treat `attention_revision_stale` as a separate prior slice, not a babysat side effect of later wake/ownership work: make commit authority tolerate peer packet/attention advancement when the staged tree and approved slice state are unchanged, as escalated in `rev_pkt_1223` and `rev_pkt_1234`.
      owner_doc: `dev/active/remote_commit_pipeline.md`
      status: `pending`
      depends_on: `MP417-P0`

### Phase MP-418 - Process Lifecycle Ownership

- [x] `MP418-T03` Project one typed loop-autonomy contract across single-agent, dual-agent, local, and remote-control modes so control-plane surfaces stop treating host wake as a remote-only special case or a dual-agent-only guard.

      status: `done`

### Phase P2 - Closed-Loop Quality Composition And Audit Orchestration

Phase metadata: phase_id=MP377-P2; owner_doc=`dev/active/review_probes.md`; status=pending; depends_on=`MP377-P0`, `MP377-P1`; summary=Turn the existing guards, probes, dogfood ledger, and planning reducers into one closed quality loop with shared inputs and consumable outputs.

- [ ] `MP377-P2-T01` Compose `probe_split_advisor.py` from function clusters, import graph fan-out, and hotspot context.

      status: `pending`
      depends_on: `MP377-P1-T01`
- [ ] `MP377-P2-T02` Feed canonical backlog findings and guard/probe outputs back into planning and findings-priority instead of parallel markdown-only lanes.

      status: `pending`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T01`

      status: `pending`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T01`
- [ ] `MP377-P2-T04` Compose a multi-agent `devctl dogfood` audit runner that exercises command/guard/probe/role coverage, persists cross-session coverage, and records real failures through `governance-review`.

      status: `pending`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T04`, `MP377-P0-T06`, `MP377-P1-T05`
- [ ] `MP377-P2-T05` Promote dogfood from ad hoc walkthrough to the development engine: persist repo-owned dev-mode coverage/results, feed them back into `FindingBacklog`, `findings-priority`, and planning reducers, and make new platform work land with a dogfood proof path instead of `/tmp` notes or operator memory.
      owner_doc: `dev/active/autonomous_governance_loop_v2.md`
      status: `pending`
      depends_on: `MP377-P0-T04`, `MP377-P0-T06`, `MP377-P1-T07`

      owner_doc: `dev/active/remote_control_runtime.md`
      status: `pending`
